title: dichrocephala chrysanthemifolia var. abyssinica
intro: Dichrocephala chrysanthemifolia var. abyssinica, also known as Abyssinian tea or false chamomile, is a plant species native to the highlands of Ethiopia and the eastern parts of the Democratic Republic of Congo. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Dichrocephala chrysanthemifolia var. abyssinica include reducing fever, relieving gastrointestinal issues, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically made into a tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal and health benefits.
